questions.question: Explain the difference between procedural programming and object-oriented programming.
keyPoints: Procedural focuses on functions/procedures; OOP focuses on objects
Procedural organizes code around data and functions separately; OOP bundles them together
OOP provides better code reusability through inheritance
Procedural is suitable for simple, straightforward programs; OOP for complex systems

questions.question: What are the four fundamental principles of OOP? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Encapsulation: Bundling data and methods into a single unit
Inheritance: Mechanism of deriving new classes from existing ones
Polymorphism: Ability to take multiple forms
Abstraction: Hiding complex details, showing only essential features

questions.question: What is a class in C++? How does it differ from a structure?
keyPoints: Class is a blueprint for creating objects
Class members are private by default; struct members are public by default
Both can have member functions, constructors, destructors
Struct is primarily for passive data containers; class for active objects

questions.question: Explain the concept of inheritance in OOP. Why is it important?
keyPoints: Inheritance allows a class to derive properties from another class
Promotes code reusability - no need to rewrite common attributes/methods
Creates hierarchical relationships between classes
Supports extensibility - new classes can extend existing functionality

questions.question: What is the difference between data abstraction and data encapsulation?
keyPoints: Abstraction focuses on hiding complex implementation details
Encapsulation focuses on bundling data and methods together
Abstraction is about 'what' something does; encapsulation is about 'how' it's done
Both promote modularity and reduce complexity

questions.question: Explain polymorphism in C++ with an example.
keyPoints: Polymorphism allows same interface for different underlying forms
Achieved through function overloading (compile-time) and virtual functions (runtime)
Example: A base class Shape with virtual function draw(), overridden in Circle, Square classes
Enables treating objects of different classes uniformly through base class pointers

questions.question: Why is C++ called a multi-paradigm language? Give examples of how it supports different paradigms.
keyPoints: C++ supports procedural programming (like C) with functions and structures
C++ supports OOP with classes, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation
C++ supports generic programming through templates
C++ supports functional programming features like lambdas and functors

questions.question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of Object-Oriented Programming over Procedural Programming. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Advantages of OOP: Better code organization, reusability through inheritance, easier maintenance, models real-world entities better, provides data security through encapsulation
Disadvantages of OOP: Steeper learning curve, can be overkill for small programs, more complex design, slightly slower execution due to overhead
Advantages of Procedural: Simpler, faster for small tasks, easier to understand flow, less memory overhead
When to choose: OOP for large, complex projects with many related entities; Procedural for simple, sequential tasks, embedded systems, performance-critical applications

questions.question: Explain the different types of inheritance in C++ with examples. Also discuss the concept of multiple inheritance and its potential problems.
keyPoints: Types: Single (one base class), Multi-level (class derived from derived class), Hierarchical (one base, multiple derived), Hybrid (combination)
Multiple inheritance: Class inherits from more than one base class
Problem: Diamond problem - ambiguity when two base classes have same method
Solution: Virtual base classes, scope resolution operator, using interfaces
Example code showing inheritance syntax and demonstration

questions.question: What are access specifiers in C++? Explain public, private, and protected with examples showing their usage in OOP.
keyPoints: Public: Accessible from anywhere - defines interface
Private: Accessible only within the class - implements data hiding
Protected: Accessible within class and derived classes - for inheritance
Default for class is private; default for struct is public
Use cases: Private for data members, public for methods, protected for member variables meant for inheritance

questions.question: Design a class hierarchy for a banking system demonstrating encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism concepts.
keyPoints: Create base class Account with attributes like balance, accountNumber
Derived classes: SavingsAccount, CurrentAccount, FixedDepositAccount
Encapsulation: Make data members private, provide public getter/setter methods
Polymorphism: Virtual function calculateInterest() overridden in each account type
Main function demonstrating object creation and polymorphism
